nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Brain is divided into hindbrain, midbrain, and forebrain
nodes.description: The brain is a complex organ that has many different parts that work together to control our thoughts, actions, and emotions, divided into three main parts: hindbrain, midbrain, and forebrain.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Hindbrain controls basic bodily functions and coordination
nodes.description: The hindbrain is responsible for controlling basic bodily functions like breathing and heart rate, and plays a key role in maintaining balance and coordination.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Midbrain processes sensory information and controls movement
nodes.description: The midbrain is involved in processing sensory information from our environment, as well as controlling movement and reflexes.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Forebrain is responsible for higher-level cognitive functions
nodes.description: The forebrain is the largest part of the brain and is responsible for higher-level cognitive functions like thinking, reasoning, and problem-solving, and contains the limbic system which regulates emotions and memory.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Different parts of the brain communicate through neural pathways
nodes.description: Different parts of the brain communicate with each other through a complex system of neural pathways, allowing them to work together to coordinate our thoughts, actions, and emotions.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Amygdala triggers fight or flight response during stress
nodes.description: When we experience a stressful situation, the amygdala in the limbic system is activated, triggering a fight or flight response.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Prefrontal cortex weighs options during fight or flight response
nodes.description: The prefrontal cortex in the forebrain weighs the options and decides whether to engage in the fight or flight response.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Motor cortex sends signals to basal ganglia for complex motor tasks
nodes.description: When performing a complex motor task like playing the piano, the motor cortex in the forebrain sends signals down to the basal ganglia in the midbrain, coordinating the movements of different muscle groups.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Brain can be trained to improve memory and decision making
nodes.description: The brain can be trained to improve its functioning in areas like memory or decision making through neuroplasticity, adapting and changing in response to its environment and experiences.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Regular exercise improves memory and increases brain volume
nodes.description: Research has shown that regular exercise can improve memory and increase brain volume in the hippocampus, a brain region critical for memory consolidation.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Practice and repetition improve skills like playing musical instruments
nodes.description: Practice and repetition can improve skills like playing a musical instrument, which involves complex sensory and motor processing in the brain.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Cognitive training programs target specific cognitive functions
nodes.description: Cognitive training programs, such as brain training games or memory training exercises, have been developed to target specific cognitive functions, with varying effectiveness.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Dual n-back training improves working memory and attention
nodes.description: Dual n-back training involves remembering a sequence of numbers and sounds simultaneously, improving working memory and attention, and potentially fluid intelligence.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Mindfulness meditation improves working memory and decision making
nodes.description: Mindfulness meditation involves focusing attention on breathing and observing thoughts and feelings, improving working memory, attention, and decision making.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Strategy games like chess can improve memory and decision making
nodes.description: Games that involve strategy and planning, such as chess, can improve cognitive functions like memory and decision making.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Effectiveness of brain training programs varies by individual
nodes.description: The effectiveness of brain training programs can vary depending on a person's age, cognitive abilities, and the specific program being used.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Set constraints express relationships between sets of predicators in a population
nodes.description: Set constraints are used to express relationships between different sets of predicators in a population, including subset, equal, and exclusion constraints.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Subset constraint specifies one set of predicators is a subset of another
nodes.description: A subset constraint specifies that one set of predicators (σ) is a subset of another set of predicators (τ) in the population, with a matching (φ) between the two sets.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Equal constraint specifies one set of predicators is equal to another
nodes.description: An equal constraint specifies that one set of predicators (σ) is equal to another set of predicators (τ) in the population, with a matching (φ) between the two sets.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Exclusion constraint specifies one set of predicators is disjoint from another
nodes.description: An exclusion constraint specifies that one set of predicators (σ) is disjoint from another set of predicators (τ) in the population, with a matching (φ) between the two sets.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Matching (φ) is a bijection between two sets of predicators
nodes.description: A matching (φ) is a bijection between two sets of predicators (σ and τ) where each element in the set (φ(x)) is type related with the corresponding element in the other set (x).

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Set constraints allow for flexibility in specifying relationships
nodes.description: Strength: Set constraints allow for more flexibility in specifying the relationships between different sets of predicators.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Set constraints allow for precise specification of relationships
nodes.description: Strength: Set constraints allow for more precise specification of the required relationships between different sets of predicators.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Set constraints help ensure data integrity
nodes.description: Strength: Set constraints can help ensure the integrity of the data by specifying certain constraints that must be met.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Set constraints may be complex to specify
nodes.description: Weakness: Set constraints may be complex to specify and can be difficult to understand for those not familiar with them.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Set constraints may be time-consuming to implement
nodes.description: Weakness: Set constraints may be time-consuming to implement and enforce in a database.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27] Incorrectly specified set constraints can lead to data inconsistencies
nodes.description: Weakness: If set constraints are not specified correctly, they can lead to incorrect results or data inconsistencies.
